topics.title: Computer Science fundamentals
learning_objectives: Build a high-level mental model of core computer science foundations before programming
Differentiate hardware, software, and development environment responsibilities
Understand major ways to run Python code and when to use each approach
topics.notes: Cover fundamental mathematics overview (number sense, calculus, statistics); hardware (storage, memory/cache, CPU, GPU); software basics (directory trees, files/permissions, command line); introduction to VS Code; intro to Python and interpreter/anaconda setup; and ways to run Python: text editor + command line, IDEs (Spyder, VS Code, RStudio), and Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb, colab).

topics.title: Introduction to base python (part-1)
learning_objectives: Write simple Python programs with arithmetic, console output, and user input
Recognize built-in data types and select suitable structures for small tasks
Understand foundational data structure concepts such as methods and attributes
topics.notes: Focus on base Python without external packages: arithmetic operations, output to console, accepting user input, built-in data types (numeric, boolean, text, None), built-in structures (list/tuple/set/dictionary), and data structure concepts (methods and attributes).

topics.title: Introduction to base python (part-2)
learning_objectives: Translate simple problem ideas into pseudocode and executable Python steps
Apply conditionals, loops, and functions to structure program flow
Handle basic file input and output reliably
topics.notes: Cover algorithmic programming (pseudocode and thinking like a programmer), control statements, control flow (if/else, for/while, graceful exits), functions (namespace/scope, arguments, return values), and IO handling (reading/writing files).

topics.title: Introduction to Numpy and Pandas
learning_objectives: Import and alias common Python data packages in a consistent style
Understand why NumPy improves numeric computation speed and array handling
Use Pandas for basic tabular data manipulation and summary statistics
topics.notes: Cover package imports/aliasing, NumPy efficiency and matrix/array work, and Pandas for data manipulation/augmentation plus basic statistics.

topics.title: Introduction to Data Visualization in Matplotlib and Seaborn
learning_objectives: Understand the pyplot plotting workflow and its relation to MATLAB-style usage
Use Matplotlib for plot structure, geometry control, and basic charting
Use Seaborn on top of Matplotlib/Pandas for quick statistical visualizations
topics.notes: Cover pyplot backend, Matplotlib structure/geometries/basic plotting, and Seaborn integration with Matplotlib and Pandas for basic plots.
